title: NovaTerm MCP API Specification
description: Universal MCP API for NovaTerm — the AI-native Android terminal. Provider-agnostic, versioned, secure, streaming-capable. Compatible with Claude Code, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Aider, Copilot CLI, Goose, Crush, Amp, Kiro, Cline, Continue, and any MCP-compliant client.

notes: Token is saved to app-private storage (mode 0600) on server start
Only accessible from same device (localhost binding)
Clients discover token via file or manual copy from Settings UI

versioning.deprecationPolicy: Major versions supported for 12 months after successor release

migration.v1-to-v2: Automatic namespace aliasing: flat tool names (v1) map to namespaced names (v2)

SAFE.description: Read-only, no side effects. Auto-approved from localhost.

MODERATE.description: Modifies state in a controlled way. Auto-approved from localhost by default, configurable.

DANGEROUS.description: Arbitrary execution, file writes, process signals. Requires explicit user approval via UI dialog.

CRITICAL.description: Irreversible destructive operations. Always requires user approval + confirmation.

mcp.description: Main MCP endpoint for tool calls and lifecycle methods

queryParams.subscribe: Comma-separated event types to subscribe to

discover.description: Returns all available tools with full schemas, risk levels, and namespace info

terminal.description: Core terminal I/O operations — read, write, and control the terminal emulator

session.description: Session lifecycle management — create, close, switch, list terminal sessions

pane.description: Split pane / multiplexer control — split, resize, swap panes within a session

fs.description: Filesystem operations — read, write, list, search files and directories

env.description: Environment variables and working directory management

process.description: Process management — list, signal, wait for running processes

ui.description: UI control — theme, font, notifications, layout for the NovaTerm interface

ai.description: AI-specific features — predictions, suggestions, semantic blocks, terminal context

system.description: Device and terminal system information — capabilities, battery, network

event.description: Event subscriptions for real-time streaming — output, command lifecycle, session events

terminal.write.name: terminal.write
terminal.write.description: Write text or raw bytes to the active terminal PTY, as if typed by the user. Supports sending control sequences (e.g., \x03 for Ctrl+C).

text.description: Text to write to the terminal PTY. Supports escape sequences (\n, \t, \x03, etc.).

session.description: Session index to write to. Defaults to the active session.

newline.description: Whether to append a newline after the text (simulates pressing Enter).

terminal.read.name: terminal.read
terminal.read.description: Read the current visible screen content from the terminal (what the user can see right now). Returns rows x cols grid of text.

session.description: Session index. Defaults to active session.

format.description: Output format: 'text' (plain text), 'ansi' (with ANSI escape codes), 'json' (structured cells with attributes).

terminal.read_scrollback.name: terminal.read_scrollback
terminal.read_scrollback.description: Read from the terminal scrollback buffer. Supports pagination with offset and limit for navigating large output history.

lines.description: Number of lines to read from scrollback.

offset.description: Offset from the bottom of scrollback (0 = most recent). For pagination.

terminal.resize.name: terminal.resize
terminal.resize.description: Resize the terminal to the specified dimensions. Sends SIGWINCH to the running process.

terminal.clear.name: terminal.clear
terminal.clear.description: Clear the terminal screen. Equivalent to sending CSI 2J + CSI H.

terminal.reset.name: terminal.reset
terminal.reset.description: Perform a full terminal reset (RIS). Resets all terminal state: modes, colors, character set, scrollback.

terminal.get_cursor.name: terminal.get_cursor
terminal.get_cursor.description: Get the current cursor position and style in the terminal.

terminal.get_dimensions.name: terminal.get_dimensions
terminal.get_dimensions.description: Get terminal dimensions: rows, columns, and cell size in pixels.

terminal.screenshot.name: terminal.screenshot
terminal.screenshot.description: Render the current terminal screen as a PNG image. Returns base64-encoded image data. Useful for visual debugging or sharing.

terminal.search.name: terminal.search
terminal.search.description: Search for a pattern in the terminal scrollback buffer. Supports plain text and regex.

terminal.run_command.name: terminal.run_command
terminal.run_command.description: Execute a shell command in a terminal session and return the output. Writes the command, waits for completion, and captures the result. This is the primary tool for AI agents to interact with the shell.

wait_ms.description: Maximum time to wait for output in milliseconds.

timeout_ms.description: Hard timeout after which the command is killed.

cwd.description: Working directory to cd into before running the command. Optional.

output.description: Command output captured from the terminal.

exit_code.description: Exit code (0 = success). Null if not determinable.

terminal.wait_for_output.name: terminal.wait_for_output
terminal.wait_for_output.description: Wait until specific output appears in the terminal, or until timeout. Useful for waiting for prompts, compilation results, or long-running processes.

pattern.description: Text or regex pattern to wait for.

session.list.name: session.list
session.list.description: List all active terminal sessions with their status, PID, title, and working directory.

session.create.name: session.create
session.create.description: Create a new terminal session. Optionally specify a working directory, shell, or initial command.

shell.description: Shell to use (e.g., 'bash', 'zsh'). Default: user's configured shell.

command.description: Initial command to run after session creation.

index.description: Index of the newly created session.

session.close.name: session.close
session.close.description: Close a terminal session. Sends SIGHUP to the shell process and cleans up resources.

force.description: Force close (SIGKILL) if session doesn't terminate within 5 seconds.

session.switch.name: session.switch
session.switch.description: Switch the active (focused) terminal session by index or title.

title.description: Session title to switch to (alternative to index).

session.get_info.name: session.get_info
session.get_info.description: Get detailed information about a specific session: PID, CWD, title, running process, shell, environment.

session.get_history.name: session.get_history
session.get_history.description: Get command history for a session. Returns the list of commands executed, with timestamps if available.

pane.split.name: pane.split
pane.split.description: Split the current pane into two, creating a new terminal within the same session view.

direction.description: Split direction. 'horizontal' creates a left/right split, 'vertical' creates a top/bottom split.

pane_id.description: Pane to split. Defaults to the focused pane.

command.description: Command to run in the new pane.

pane_id.description: ID of the newly created pane.

pane.close.name: pane.close
pane.close.description: Close a specific pane. The sibling pane expands to fill the space.

pane.focus.name: pane.focus
pane.focus.description: Focus a specific pane, making it the active input target.

pane.list.name: pane.list
pane.list.description: List the pane tree for a session, showing the layout hierarchy, sizes, and focused pane.

pane.resize.name: pane.resize
pane.resize.description: Resize a pane by adjusting its split ratio relative to its sibling.

pane.swap.name: pane.swap
pane.swap.description: Swap the positions of two panes.

fs.read.name: fs.read
fs.read.description: Read the content of a file. Supports text files with optional line range. Binary files are returned as base64.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the file.

line_start.description: Start reading from this line (1-indexed). For partial reads.

fs.write.name: fs.write
fs.write.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Supports creating parent directories.

append.description: Append to file instead of overwriting.

create_dirs.description: Create parent directories if they don't exist.

path.description: Absolute path of the written file.

fs.list.name: fs.list
fs.list.description: List directory contents with file metadata (size, permissions, modified time).

fs.stat.name: fs.stat
fs.stat.description: Get detailed file or directory information: size, permissions, timestamps, type, symlink target.

fs.delete.name: fs.delete
fs.delete.description: Delete a file or empty directory. For recursive deletion, set recursive=true (CRITICAL risk).

recursive.description: Delete directory and all contents. Elevates to CRITICAL risk.

fs.move.name: fs.move
fs.move.description: Move or rename a file or directory.

fs.search.name: fs.search
fs.search.description: Search for files by name pattern (glob) or content (grep). Combines find + grep functionality.

content_pattern.description: Regex pattern to search in file contents.

fs.copy.name: fs.copy
fs.copy.description: Copy a file or directory to a new location.

env.get.name: env.get
env.get.description: Get the value of an environment variable from a session.

env.set.name: env.set
env.set.description: Set an environment variable in a session. The variable persists for the session lifetime.

env.list.name: env.list
env.list.description: List all environment variables in a session.

filter.description: Glob pattern to filter variable names (e.g., 'PATH*', 'ANDROID_*').

env.get_cwd.name: env.get_cwd
env.get_cwd.description: Get the current working directory of a session.

env.set_cwd.name: env.set_cwd
env.set_cwd.description: Change the working directory in a session.

path.description: Directory to change to. Supports ~ for home.

process.list.name: process.list
process.list.description: List running processes visible to the terminal user. Equivalent to 'ps aux' but structured.

filter.description: Filter by process name or command.

session.description: Show only processes in this session's process group.

process.signal.name: process.signal
process.signal.description: Send a signal to a process (SIGINT, SIGTERM, SIGKILL, SIGSTOP, SIGCONT, etc.).

process.wait.name: process.wait
process.wait.description: Wait for a process to exit. Returns the exit code when the process terminates.

signal.description: Signal that caused exit, if any.

process.get_exit_code.name: process.get_exit_code
process.get_exit_code.description: Get the exit code of the last command executed in a session (via $?).

ui.set_theme.name: ui.set_theme
ui.set_theme.description: Change the terminal color scheme. Takes effect immediately (hot-reload).

rows.description: New row count after font change.

cols.description: New column count after font change.

ui.get_preferences.name: ui.get_preferences
ui.get_preferences.description: Get all current UI settings: theme, font, layout, extra keys config.

ui.notify.name: ui.notify
ui.notify.description: Show an Android notification from the terminal. Useful for long-running task completion alerts.

ui.toast.name: ui.toast
ui.toast.description: Show a brief toast message on screen.

ui.set_layout.name: ui.set_layout
ui.set_layout.description: Control UI layout: toggle extra keys bar, status line, fullscreen mode.

ai.suggest.name: ai.suggest
ai.suggest.description: Get N-gram based command suggestions given the current context (previous command, CWD, typing prefix). Uses the on-device prediction engine.

prefix.description: What the user is currently typing.

ai.predict.name: ai.predict
ai.predict.description: Predict the next likely command based on learned patterns. Uses N-gram model + optional NPU inference.

ai.explain.name: ai.explain
ai.explain.description: Get an explanation of the last command output or a specific block. Uses on-device LLM if available, otherwise returns structured data for the calling AI to interpret.

block_id.description: Specific semantic block ID to explain. If omitted, explains the last command.

ai.get_blocks.name: ai.get_blocks
ai.get_blocks.description: Get semantic blocks from OSC 133 zones. Each block contains a command prompt, input, and output, providing structured access to terminal history.

limit.description: Number of recent blocks to return.

include_output.description: Include command output in each block.

ai.get_context.name: ai.get_context
ai.get_context.description: Get a comprehensive terminal context snapshot optimized for AI consumption. Includes recent commands, CWD, environment highlights, git status, running processes, and capabilities. This is the recommended first call for any AI agent connecting to NovaTerm.

environment.description: Key environment variables (PATH, HOME, PREFIX, TERM, etc.).

system.info.name: system.info
system.info.description: Get device and system information: Android version, device model, available RAM, storage.

system.capabilities.name: system.capabilities
system.capabilities.description: Get terminal and device capabilities. AI agents use this to determine what features are available (truecolor, Kitty keyboard, Vulkan, etc.).

system.battery.name: system.battery
system.battery.description: Get battery status: level, charging state, temperature, health.

system.network.name: system.network
system.network.description: Get network connectivity status: WiFi, mobile data, IP addresses.

event.subscribe.name: event.subscribe
event.subscribe.description: Subscribe to real-time terminal events via SSE. Returns a subscription ID that can be used to filter the SSE stream at /mcp/v1/events.

session.description: Filter events to a specific session. Omit for all sessions.

sse_url.description: SSE endpoint URL with subscription filter.

events.description: Real-time event stream via Server-Sent Events (SSE) at GET /mcp/v1/events
events.authentication: Bearer token in query param (?token=...) or Authorization header
events.reconnect: Clients should reconnect on disconnect with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, max 30s)

output_received.description: New output appeared in a terminal session.

command_started.description: A command began execution (detected via OSC 133).

command_done.description: A command finished execution with an exit code.

session_created.description: A new terminal session was created.

title_changed.description: Session title was updated (via OSC 0/2 or API).

error.description: An error occurred in the terminal or MCP server.

discovery.description: Self-describing API discovery. Returns all available tools with full schemas, organized by namespace.

response.tools: Complete tool catalog with inputSchema, outputSchema, riskLevel
response.events: Available event types for SSE subscriptions

integration.description: How AI coding tools connect to NovaTerm's MCP server.

mcp_handshake.description: Standard MCP protocol handshake (JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP).
steps: 2. Client reads bearer token from $APP_PRIVATE_DIR/files/mcp-token or user provides it
3. Client sends 'initialize' request with client capabilities
4. Server responds with server capabilities and protocol version
6. Client calls 'tools/list' to discover available tools
7. Client calls tools via 'tools/call' with tool name and arguments
8. Optionally, client subscribes to SSE events at /mcp/v1/events

notes: Claude Code supports MCP over streamable HTTP natively
Token can be read from the mcp-token file or configured manually
All tools appear as callable functions in Claude Code's tool catalog
Claude Code's permission system integrates with NovaTerm's risk levels
OpenCode supports MCP servers via streamable HTTP
Configure in project-level or global config
Gemini CLI supports MCP via stdio transport (use mcp-remote bridge for HTTP)
Alternative: direct HTTP support if Gemini CLI adds streamable HTTP transport

aider.setup: Configure MCP server in aider config when MCP support is available
notes: Aider may require a bridge tool for MCP integration
Use terminal.run_command as primary interaction tool
Copilot CLI may add MCP support in future versions
Meanwhile, use the HTTP API directly via curl or a bridge script

generic_http.description: Any tool can connect via standard HTTP + JSON-RPC 2.0

auto_discovery.description: NovaTerm can auto-configure MCP clients running inside the terminal.
mechanism: AI tools that support env-based MCP discovery will auto-connect

v0_to_v1_migration.description: Migration from the current flat tool names to namespaced tools.
v0_to_v1_migration.strategy: Old flat names are accepted as aliases and mapped to namespaced names. Deprecated in v1, removed in v2.
